behavior_summary: U8P_BELLOWS::look is the inspection handler for the Bellows item: when the player 'looks at' the item it emits a short bark/text label "bellows" and the U8P_BELLOWS::look process then suspends.

U8P_BELLOWS::use is the runtime 'U8P_BELLOWS::use' handler for the Bellows. It reads the item's current frame (Item::getFrame(this)), chooses one of two frame ranges (0x00–0x05 or 0x06–0x0B) based on that frame, sets the visible frame (Item::setFrame(local1, this)), spawns a short transient effect/task (spawn -> FREE::func1C19), suspends briefly to allow the effect/animation to run, conditionally plays a sound (playSFX with id 0x64) when particular frame indices are reached (inferred 0x02 or 0x08), and increments a local frame/index (local1) for the next U8P_BELLOWS::use.
No global flags are read or written by these routines in the extracted code.
notable_control_flow: U8P_BELLOWS::look: linear single-action flow — bark then suspend.

U8P_BELLOWS::use: branch on current Item::getFrame(this) into two frame-range handling paths; each path initializes a local index, bounds-checks it against the range, calls Item::setFrame, spawns an effect, suspends, conditionally plays SFX for specific indices, then increments the index.
Use contains multiple IfStatement checks around the frame index and at least two SetFlag/StringAssign style operations in the decompilation remnants, but no persistent global flags are set.
open_questions: Exact wrap/clamping behavior of the local frame/index (local1) across uses — whether it wraps back to the range start or stops at the end is inferred but not explicit.
Precise semantics and parameters of the spawned task FREE::func1C19 (visual effect, timing, ownership) and how its pid is tracked — present as low-confidence raw artifacts.
Whether there are other entry points that alter the Bellows' frame outside these processes (not visible in this excerpt).

main_function.name: U8P_BELLOWS::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short inspection/description when the player examines the Bellows (emit a short bark/label and then suspend).
entry_conditions: Triggered when the player performs a 'U8P_BELLOWS::look' or 'examine' action on the Bellows item.
Acts as the object's U8P_BELLOWS::look/inspect event handler (engine U8P_BELLOWS::look-type process).

function_map.name: U8P_BELLOWS::look
function_map.role: inspection/U8P_BELLOWS::look handler that shows the short label/bark to the player and ends the process

function_map.name: U8P_BELLOWS::use
function_map.role: U8P_BELLOWS::use-handler that advances/sets the item's visual frame, spawns a transient effect, possibly plays SFX, and advances an internal frame index

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: external API to read an item instance's current frame/index (called by U8P_BELLOWS::use)

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: external API to set an item instance's visible frame/index (called by U8P_BELLOWS::use)

function_map.name: FREE::func1C19 (spawned)
function_map.role: transient spawned task/effect invoked by U8P_BELLOWS::use (visual/temporal effect inferred)

function_map.name: playSFX
function_map.role: plays a sound effect when certain frame indices are reached during U8P_BELLOWS::use

external_calls.purpose: Read the Bellows' current animation/frame index to decide which frame-range branch to run.
Apply the computed/selected frame index (local1) to the Bellows so its visible state advances/changes.
Spawn a transient task/effect (likely visual or timing-related) immediately after setting the frame; used to drive or accompany the animation.
Pause the U8P_BELLOWS::use process briefly to allow the spawned effect/animation to play.
Play audible feedback (SFX id 0x64, flags 0x10) when the frame index reaches particular values (inferred 0x02 or 0x08).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: U8P_BELLOWS::look is the inspection handler for the Bellows item: when the player 'looks at' the item it emits a short bark/text label "bellows" and the U8P_BELLOWS::look process then suspends.
